instruction:
(a) The graph of $y=2^{x-3}$ is the graph of $y=2^{x}$ $\begin{array}{ccc}\hline shifted horizontally & & [ANS]right 3 unitsright 3 unitsright 4 unitsright 4 unitsright 5 unitsright 5 unitsand \\ \hline shifted vertically & & [ANS]up 3 unitsup 3 unitsup 4 unitsup 4 unitsup 5 unitsup 5 units. \\ \hline \end{array}$
(b) The graph of $y=e^{x}+1$ is the graph of $y=e^{x}$ $\begin{array}{ccc}\hline shifted horizontally & & [ANS]right 3 unitsright 3 unitsright 4 unitsright 4 unitsright 5 unitsright 5 unitsand \\ \hline shifted vertically & & [ANS]up 3 unitsup 3 unitsup 4 unitsup 4 unitsup 5 unitsup 5 units. \\ \hline \end{array}$
(c) The graph of $y=2^{x-1}+2$ is the graph of $y=2^{x}$ $\begin{array}{ccc}\hline shifted horizontally & & [ANS]right 3 unitsright 3 unitsright 4 unitsright 4 unitsright 5 unitsright 5 unitsand \\ \hline shifted vertically & & [ANS]up 3 unitsup 3 unitsup 4 unitsup 4 unitsup 5 unitsup 5 units. \\ \hline \end{array}$
(d) The graph of $y=e^{x+2}+9$ is the graph of $y=e^{x}+10$ $\begin{array}{ccc}\hline shifted horizontally & & [ANS]right 3 unitsright 3 unitsright 4 unitsright 4 unitsright 5 unitsright 5 unitsand \\ \hline shifted vertically & & [ANS]up 3 unitsup 3 unitsup 4 unitsup 4 unitsup 5 unitsup 5 units. \\ \hline \end{array}$
output: right 3 units, no vertical shift, no horizontal shift, up 1 unit, right 1 unit, up 2 units, left 2 units, down 1 unit
